sections.title: Reading Passage 1
sections.instructions: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-6.

blocks.label: A
blocks.text: The excavation at Kalambo Falls sits on a terrace above the river, where waterlogged deposits have preserved worked timber that would normally have rotted away within a few seasons.

blocks.label: B
blocks.text: Halvorsen argued that the original dating was wrong, pointing out that the samples had been taken from a layer disturbed by later flooding.

blocks.label: C
blocks.text: Ibarra developed a new measurement technique based on luminescence, which allowed the surrounding sand to be dated directly rather than relying on organic material.

blocks.label: D
blocks.text: Nakamura questioned the value of further fieldwork at the site, noting that the cost of each season had risen sharply while the return in new material had fallen.

groups.instructions: Look at the following statements and the list of researchers below. Match each statement with the correct researcher, A-C.

questions.prompt: argued that the original dating was wrong.
questioned the value of further fieldwork.

groups.instructions: Which paragraph contains the following information? Write the correct letter, A-D.

questions.prompt: a description of the excavation site
